dimensions.evidence: Plan requires 5 specific tests; delivered: Test 1 (UnsupportedLanguage) is t.Skip'd, Test 2 (UnmeasuredComplexity) documents bug instead of asserting fix, Test 3 (SupportedLanguage) has gamed conditions and is truncated, Test 4 (DefaultsNotInjected) is entirely missing, Test 5 (AggregateSkipsUnsupported) is t.Skip'd.
All changes are confined to internal/engine/scorer_test.go as the plan specified; no production files or other non-target files touched.

TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks uses conjunction conditions (grade==0 && avg<0.60; status==0 && avg<0.80) that avoid failure for many edge cases — a gamed assertion. Two tests are t.Skip'd (no test executed). UnmeasuredComplexity test accepts buggy 0.95 and only logs, never fails. Missing DefaultsNotInjected test entirely.

No production code changed, so no direct regression/security risk. However, gamed and skipped tests give false confidence that the plan was fulfilled; the missing defaults test and bug-documenting test leave the plan's safety goals unmet.

failureReasons: Only 1 of 5 required tests has a non-skipped, non-gamed implementation (and even it is truncated/gamed). TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA is t.Skip'd. TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported is t.Skip'd. TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected is entirely missing. TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScoredExcellent documents the bug as accepted behavior instead of asserting the fix. TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks uses conjunctive conditions that avoid failure under many outcomes, constituting test gaming.
